objective: Define release manifest, checksum inventory, archive boundary and deterministic fingerprint
acceptance: Schema rejects missing, duplicate, secret-bearing, mutable or non-deterministic release metadata
validation: typecheck;focused schema tests;manifest determinism test

objective: Build a versioned private release directory and archive from source, build, cockpit and migration evidence
acceptance: Two builds from identical source produce identical manifest and file checksums; archive excludes secrets, runtime state, databases and artifacts
validation: focused archive tests;fresh extraction build;checksum parity
blockers: missing required build tool or source evidence must fail closed

objective: Implement Node 20, PostgreSQL, disk, path and private configuration presence preflight without outputting values
acceptance: Preflight reports presence and compatibility only, redacts all credentials and rejects partial or ambiguous configuration
validation: success and failure matrix;secret-output scan
blockers: private server credentials are not required for unit proof

objective: Add non-privileged operator user-service templates for the product-owned lifecycle
acceptance: Templates bind to exact release directory, private EnvironmentFile, loopback runtime and exact lifecycle commands; autonomous validation does not install or enable services
validation: template parser tests;forbidden command scan;path substitution test
blockers: systemd may be unavailable in CI, so validation is static and non-mutating

objective: Implement non-mutating install verification and immutable release evidence publication
acceptance: Verification checks archive, manifest, executable modes, Node/PostgreSQL compatibility, templates and closed boundaries without starting services or changing the database
validation: fresh extraction proof;tamper tests;npm run validate

objective: Define exact current/target release identities, migration compatibility and upgrade decision schema
acceptance: Upgrade plan is deterministic, explicit and blocked when source, backup, migration or ownership evidence is missing
validation: plan determinism;compatibility matrix;tamper tests

objective: Implement pre-upgrade backup gate, exact-owner drain, target install verification and migration apply flow
acceptance: Apply requires explicit plan fingerprint, verified backup, exact lifecycle ownership and target release; no destructive fallback
validation: disposable release and PostgreSQL integration;interruption tests

objective: Implement safe rollback decision and interrupted-upgrade recovery state machine
acceptance: Rollback is allowed only when migration and backup evidence prove compatibility; unsafe rollback remains stopped with actionable evidence
validation: rollback allowed/blocked matrix;crash checkpoints;restart tests

objective: Prove disposable restore, exact-version recovery and immutable upgrade evidence
acceptance: Interrupted deployment, failed readiness, failed migration and stale lifecycle state recover without touching active project databases
validation: disposable database proof;fresh release directories;npm run validate

objective: Define deterministic soak campaign schema, checkpointing and resumable harness
acceptance: Campaign requires explicit duration, interval, max cycles, seed, mode and release fingerprint; resume rejects mismatched identity
validation: schema tests;resume mismatch tests;bounded accelerated integration

objective: Implement bounded failure injectors for upstream, database, scheduler, worker, API, cockpit, supervisor, backup and upgrade paths
acceptance: Every injection is explicit, loopback or disposable, uniquely owned, recoverable and retained in evidence; no broad process kill
validation: failure matrix tests;cleanup verification;zero provider/execution access scan

objective: Run and retain a real multi-hour closed-stack loopback soak campaign
acceptance: Canonical server proof runs at least 2h with deterministic checkpoints, restarts, probes, metrics, database summaries and final archive hash
validation: 2h server campaign;post-campaign invariant validator;artifact checksum
blockers: needs cycle timeout greater than 2h and disposable PostgreSQL

objective: Verify cleanup, no leaked processes, no stale leases, no execution/provider access and complete evidence publication
acceptance: All campaign-owned children exit, temporary databases and files are removed, retained evidence remains immutable and referenced
validation: post-run ownership scan;database cleanup;artifact index validation

objective: Implement exactly-one-mode accepted input preflight against the committed-HEAD upstream lock
acceptance: Export mode verifies immutable path/SHA/contract/profile/generation/lineage; API mode verifies approved read-only base URL and bounded policy; no fallback
validation: loopback export/API fixtures;mismatch matrix;no provider credentials
blockers: operator-approved external input is not needed for local implementation

objective: Validate private database, loopback service, release, backup, evidence storage and closed-boundary configuration presence
acceptance: Output contains keys and status only, never secret values or complete URLs; partial config fails closed
validation: redaction tests;partial configuration matrix

objective: Generate bws.external_runtime_campaign.v1 manifest and semantic fingerprint
acceptance: Manifest is deterministic, exact-mode, exact-release, exact-lock and references retained backup/soak evidence; check-only starts no campaign
validation: manifest determinism;tamper tests;no-start proof
blockers: real BWS-600 run remains externally blocked

objective: Create a clean release extraction and run install, preflight, migration and exact source/upstream verification
acceptance: Fresh extraction is self-contained, secret-free and checksum-clean; disposable database setup and cleanup pass
validation: clean-room acceptance stage 1

objective: Run full-stack start, status, progress, logs, paper evaluation and bounded paper-autopilot source-fix/re-evaluation proof
acceptance: Exact repo-owned lifecycle remains loopback-only, parent-only Telegram contract is preserved and no unrelated process is touched
validation: clean-room acceptance stage 2;child ownership proof

objective: Run backup, restore verification, upgrade, rollback decision and interrupted recovery proof
acceptance: All recovery paths use disposable targets and exact release identities; unsafe rollback blocks truthfully
validation: clean-room acceptance stage 3

objective: Bind soak, failure recovery, release, runtime and BWS-600 handoff evidence into final local acceptance
acceptance: Single immutable acceptance manifest references every proof, verifies cleanup, keeps execution/provider access closed and advances ledger only after npm run validate
validation: clean-room acceptance stage 4;manifest parity;npm run validate
